mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending power and rising recession probability, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with a positive mean 20-day return observed in >60% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals resilient manufacturing output that offsets rate hike concerns, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods are followed by negative 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate profit margins, which consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical relationship with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 210, elevated inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 210 are followed by positive 60-day returns in >65% of observations, invalidating the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates creates an optimal macro environment where corporate earnings expand without discount rate pressure, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of rising GDPC1 and falling FEDFUNDS fails to generate positive 60-day returns, with mean returns turning negative in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary downturn, a rising unemployment rate accelerates credit defaults and margin compression, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases during recessions are followed by positive 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample tests.
